name: AI Coding Agents
description: AI agents that write and edit code inside real codebases — CLI agents, IDE-integrated agents, editor extensions, and autonomous cloud agents, compared across form factor, model flexibility, agentic depth, trust, and pricing

groups.name: General Information
groups.description: Vendor, lifecycle status, license, and basic identifying information

attributes.name: Developer
attributes.description: Company or org that builds the agent (Anthropic, OpenAI, Cursor/Anysphere, Google, GitHub, etc.)

attributes.name: First Release
attributes.description: First public release or general-availability date (use the public announcement if GA slipped)

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status; one primary status

attributes.name: License
attributes.description: License of the agent itself, not its model

attributes.name: Specialization
attributes.description: What the agent is optimized for; most are general-purpose. Multi-select

attributes.name: Website
attributes.description: Official product page or repository

groups.name: Form & Integration
groups.description: Where the agent runs and its fundamental product shape

attributes.name: Interface
attributes.description: Product shape; select all that apply (some ship in multiple forms)

attributes.name: Host IDEs
attributes.description: Where it actually runs; standalone for forked editors (Cursor, Windsurf)

attributes.name: Open Source
attributes.description: Is the agent's own source code openly licensed? (Not the model.)

attributes.name: Self-Contained vs Plugin
attributes.description: The fundamental product shape

groups.name: Models & Flexibility
groups.description: Model provider and how much model freedom the user has

attributes.name: Default Model Provider
attributes.description: The provider behind the default/flagship model

attributes.name: Model Choice
attributes.description: Can the user switch between multiple models/providers from within the agent?

attributes.name: Bring-Your-Own-Key
attributes.description: Can the user supply their own API key instead of the vendor's billing?

attributes.name: Local Model Support
attributes.description: Can it run against a local/self-hosted model (Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM, etc.)?

groups.name: Agentic Capabilities
groups.description: What the agent can do on its own inside a codebase

attributes.name: Multi-file Editing
attributes.description: Can it plan and apply coordinated edits across multiple files in one task?

attributes.name: Autonomous Task Execution
attributes.description: Can it execute a multi-step task end-to-end with minimal per-step prompting?

attributes.name: Terminal/Command Execution
attributes.description: Can it run shell commands / build / test as part of its loop?

attributes.name: MCP Support
attributes.description: Supports the Model Context Protocol for external tools/data sources

attributes.name: Subagents / Parallel Agents
attributes.description: Can it spawn or orchestrate multiple agents (sub-tasks, parallel workers)?

attributes.name: Codebase Indexing
attributes.description: Builds a semantic index / embeddings of the repo for retrieval (vs reading files ad hoc)

attributes.name: Planning Mode
attributes.description: Has an explicit plan-then-execute or read-only planning phase

attributes.name: Skills
attributes.description: Supports reusable, packaged capabilities the agent loads on demand (skills, custom commands, recipes) beyond plain instruction files

attributes.name: Multimodal Input
attributes.description: Accepts images as input — screenshots, mockups, design files (Figma-to-code, screenshot-driven debugging)

attributes.name: Browser / Computer Use
attributes.description: Can drive a real browser or GUI to verify its own changes (navigate, click, run e2e checks, screenshot the result)

groups.name: Interaction & Autonomy Model
groups.description: How the agent engages the developer — from inline pair-programming to fire-and-forget delegation

attributes.name: Operating Mode
attributes.description: The interaction styles the agent supports; multi-select

attributes.name: Inline Completion
attributes.description: Tab-style autocomplete suggestions as you type (the interactive end). CLI agents typically lack this

attributes.name: Background / Async Execution
attributes.description: Can run a task asynchronously while the developer does other work (cloud agents, background runs)

attributes.name: Persistent Memory
attributes.description: Retains context/memory across sessions (project memory, learned preferences) rather than starting fresh each time

attributes.name: Checkpoints / Rollback
attributes.description: Can undo or restore the working tree to a point before an agent run (built-in checkpoints or snapshot restore)

groups.name: Workflow & Collaboration
groups.description: Git, pull requests, project rules, and extensibility

attributes.name: Git Integration
attributes.description: Native awareness of git (staging, diffs, commits) beyond just editing files

attributes.name: PR Creation
attributes.description: Can open or update pull requests (directly or via GitHub/GitLab integration)

attributes.name: Custom Rules / Instructions
attributes.description: Supports project-level instruction files (rules, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, etc.)

attributes.name: Hooks / Extensibility
attributes.description: Programmable hooks, custom commands/skills, or a plugin API to extend behavior

groups.name: Trust & Safety
groups.description: Permission model, self-hosting, privacy, and enterprise governance

attributes.name: Permission Model
attributes.description: How edits and command execution are gated

attributes.name: Self-Host / On-Prem
attributes.description: Can the agent be deployed on the user's own infrastructure

attributes.name: Privacy Mode
attributes.description: Vendor offers a mode that guarantees your code is not used to train models

attributes.name: Enterprise Controls
attributes.description: Enterprise-grade governance features offered

groups.name: Pricing
groups.description: Pricing model, free tier, and entry cost

attributes.name: Pricing Model
attributes.description: Select all that apply

attributes.name: Free Tier
attributes.description: Is there a usable free tier or free plan (not just a trial)?

attributes.name: Entry Price (USD/mo)
attributes.description: Cheapest paid plan's monthly price; lower is better. Use 0 if a real free tier covers core use; null for pure usage-based with no plan

groups.name: Adoption & Ecosystem
groups.description: Maturity, community size, and third-party ecosystem

attributes.name: Maturity
attributes.description: 5 = stable, widely deployed, polished; 3 = usable but evolving; 1 = early/experimental

attributes.name: Community Size
attributes.description: Relative scale of users/community

attributes.name: Extension/Plugin Ecosystem
attributes.description: Has a marketplace or meaningful third-party ecosystem (MCP servers, plugins, community rules)
